titleStmt.title: SII 18.26: original edition by N. Lakshminarayan Rao
No. 26. (B.K. No. 152 of 1933-34.) BIJĀPUR, BIJAPUR TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

summary.p: This pillar contains three inscriptions, engraved on all its sides. The first of them, in Nāgarī characters and Sanskrit language, refers itself to the reign of Akālavarsha (Kṛishṇa III), son of Amōghavarsha. It is dated in Śaka 867, Plavaṅga, Bhādrapada, Piṭriparva, Kujavāra (Tuesday), Solar eclipse. In Epigraphia Indica, Volume IV, page 58, where this record has been edited, Kielhorn gives the English equivalent as A.D. 945, September 9, Tuesday. It records a grant of land made by the Brāhmaṇa Chakrāyudha, son of Gōvindabhaṭṭa, of Pāviṭṭage, to the śālā (School) of that place constructed by Gajāṅkuśa-Nārāyaṇa, the minister of Kṛishṇarāja. Nārāyaṇa was the resident of Kamchinamuduvoḷal in the Māhisha-vishaya. Pāviṭṭage is stated to be in the Karṇapurī-vishaya.
The second inscription states that the śālā referred to above was rebuilt by Kañchiga of the Seḷaṟa family. This record is not dated.

The third one which is also not dated, records a grant of land to the dog Trayīpurushadēva of the śālā of agrahāra Pāviṭṭage, by Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Gōvaṇarasa of the Śilāhāra family. It is engraved in characters of 11-12th century A.D.
All these inscriptions are published in Epigraphia Indica, Vol. IV, pp. 57 ff. The following corrections may be made in the published texts:—
A. First face, in line 21, for Kāṁchanamuduvōl = iti read Kaṁchina-Muduvoḷal = iti. The footnote 8 is not necessary.
C. First face, in line 15, for Bāḍaḷe read bāḍada. In line 18, for ḷa-kōlal-innūṟu read da-kōlal = innūṟu and for Keyyu ma[gau] read Keyyuma[gō].
Second face, in lines 20-21, for nelanū Makiṟi-yiṇṭiya read nelanuma Kiṟi-Yiṇḍiya. In lines 24 and 25, for ti[ssa]ḷaḍaṇṭakke read Tiguḷa-daṇḍakke.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p: Digital edition of SII 18.26 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
